content.json:
# mdk - daily insights workbook (what happened & what to look at)

**Persona:** lead / on-call reviewing the day ("what changed, which project is unhealthy, what are the top 3 things to look at?").

This is the **Azure Monitor equivalent** of the insight-fed Grafana dashboards (`dashboards/grafana/insights/`). It is the intelligence layer on top of the #518 **operator** workbook (`infra/azure-monitor/workbooks/operator.workbook.json`): a narrative section, health gauges, a **Top 3** list, and anomaly-annotated golden-signal panels. Scope it to the Log Analytics workspace the deployment writes to (`logs.outputs.workspaceId` in `infra/azure/main.bicep`).

**Two data sources, on purpose.**

1. **Raw OTel metrics** - KQL against the workspace `AppMetrics` / `AppDependencies` tables the OTel Collector's `azuremonitor` exporter writes (ADR 020). These work **today**, exactly like the #518 workbooks.
2. **The intelligence layer** (narrative digest, health scores, anomalies, top failures) - comes from the **ADR 047** Observability Intelligence API (`GET /api/v1/observability/insights`), **not** from an App Insights table. See the gap banner in the next section for how to feed it.

**Cross-surface.** PromQL/Grafana side: `dashboards/grafana/insights/mdk-daily-insights.json` + `mdk-project-health.json`. Raw-metric Azure side: the #518 `operator` / `platform` / `eval-and-drift` workbooks.

> ## Gap banner - the intelligence layer needs ADR 047 (not on `main` yet)
>
> The **narrative digest**, **health scores**, **anomalies**, and **top failures** below are produced by the ADR 047 `observability_insights` store and exposed at `GET /api/v1/observability/{insights,health}`. **There is no App Insights / Log Analytics table that holds them** - so this workbook does **not** fabricate a KQL query against a non-existent table for those sections.
>
> **Realistic options to feed the intelligence sections (pick one):**
>
> 1. **Custom-endpoint query (preferred, once ADR 047 ships).** Use a workbook **ARM Actions / Custom Endpoint** query item (`queryType: 10`) pointed at `https://<runtime-host>/api/v1/observability/insights` with the `Authorization: Bearer <token>` header. The placeholder query items below are pre-shaped for this - swap the `query` JSON's `url` to your runtime host and supply the token via a workbook **parameter** (`insightsToken`).
> 2. **Paste-the-digest (works today, zero deps).** The operator runs `mdk observability digest --json` (ADR 047 CLI) and pastes the markdown into the **Narrative** parameter at the top; the narrative section renders it. No live call.
> 3. **Persist insights to a custom table.** If you configure ADR 047 to also emit insights as custom logs (e.g. `MdkInsights_CL`), replace the placeholder query items with KQL against that table. **Do this only if that table actually exists in your workspace** - otherwise leave the placeholder.
>
> Until you choose one, the intelligence sections show this banner + the placeholder text. **The raw-metric sections (latency / errors / throughput) work today** against `AppMetrics` / `AppDependencies`.

parameters.name: Project
parameters.description: Project to scope the health + top-3 sections to. Backed by the projects seen in AppMetrics (the `project` custom dimension on mdk.* instruments).
parameters.query:
AppMetrics
| where Name startswith "mdk."
| extend project = tostring(Properties["project"])
| where isnotempty(project)
| distinct project
| order by project asc

parameters.name: Narrative
parameters.description: Paste the output of `mdk observability digest --json` (.narrative_digest markdown) here to render the narrative section without a live API call (gap-banner option 2).

parameters.name: insightsToken
parameters.description: Bearer token for GET /api/v1/observability/insights, used by the custom-endpoint query items (gap-banner option 1). Leave blank to use the paste-the-digest path.

links.linkLabel: Open the #518 operator workbook (raw golden signals)
Open Azure Monitor Alerts blade (item-27 SLO rules)

content.json:
## 1. Narrative digest (yesterday / watch)

**What.** The ADR 047 `narrative_digest` markdown - a short "Yesterday: ... Watch: ..." summary. Rendered from the **Narrative** parameter above (paste-the-digest path) or from the custom-endpoint query (option 1).

**Normal.** Reads "nominal"; no SLO mentions.

**If red, do.** Use it as the index into the rest of the workbook - whatever project / signal it calls out, jump to that section below.

_Awaiting the digest. Paste `mdk observability digest --json` output into the **Narrative** parameter above, or wire the custom-endpoint query (gap-banner option 1). Until ADR 047 ships, no digest exists - this is expected._

## 2. Project health scores (0-100; green >= 80, amber 50-79, red < 50)

**What.** Per-project composite `health_score` from `GET /api/v1/observability/health` (ADR 047). The tiles use the same color map as the Grafana gauges.

**Normal.** All tiles green (>= 80).

**If red, do.** Note the red/amber project, then go to the **Top 3** section for it.

> **This section needs ADR 047** (custom-endpoint query item below). It is a **placeholder** until the endpoint exists - no fabricated KQL against a non-existent health table.

content.title: Project health_score (ADR 047 - GET /api/v1/observability/health)
content.noDataMessage: No health data. Set insightsToken + the runtime host URL in this query (gap-banner option 1), or wait for ADR 047 to ship. This is a placeholder, not a misconfiguration.

content.json:
## 3. Top 3 to look at today (anomalies + top failures, by severity)

**What.** The merged `anomalies[]` + `top_failures[]` from `GET /api/v1/observability/insights`, sorted by severity, capped at 3. Each row carries a representative `trace_id` (paste into App Insights transaction search) and `run_id`.

**Normal.** Empty or one low-severity row.

**If red, do.** Triage exactly like the operator workbook: latency anomaly -> latency heatmap (section 5) + Jaeger by `kind`; error cluster -> recent-failures (section 4) + Langfuse replay; cost anomaly -> token volume + `mdk eval`.

> **This section needs ADR 047** (custom-endpoint query below). Placeholder until the endpoint exists.

content.title: Top 3 to look at (ADR 047 - GET /api/v1/observability/insights .watchlist)
content.noDataMessage: No watchlist data. Set insightsToken + the runtime host URL in this query, or wait for ADR 047. Placeholder, not a misconfiguration.

content.json:
## 4. Recent failures (raw - works today)

**What.** Failed `agent.execute` / `workflow.execute` in the window from `AppDependencies` (ADR 020). This is the **ground-truth** behind the Top-3 error clusters. The `OperationId` pastes into App Insights transaction search.

**Normal.** Empty or a trickle of non-retried provider 5xx.

**If red, do.** Concentrated on one `ResultCode` -> provider-side; spread across codes after a deploy -> revert/pin (correlate with Container Apps revisions in the platform workbook); KB-heavy agents predominate -> open `eval-and-drift`.

content.query:
AppDependencies
| where AppRoleName == "movate-runtime" and Success == false
| where Name in ("agent.execute", "workflow.execute")
| extend project = tostring(Properties["project"])
| where isempty("{Project}") or project == "{Project}"
| project TimeGenerated, project, Name, DurationMs, ResultCode, OperationId, AppRoleInstance
| order by TimeGenerated desc
| take 100
content.title: Recent failed agent.execute / workflow.execute (last 100)

content.json:
## 5. Latency with deploy markers (raw - works today)

**What.** `agent.execute` `DurationMs` p95 over time (`AppDependencies`), the ground truth the latency anomalies are detected against. Overlay your CalVer deploy times mentally from the Container Apps revision history (the platform workbook lists them) - the Grafana equivalent draws them as annotations automatically from the insights `deploys[]`.

**Normal.** p95 under ~2000 ms.

**If red, do.** p95 > 30000 ms -> the `*-high-latency-p95` SLO is firing; line it up against the most recent revision rollout; if a model regression is suspected open `eval-and-drift`.

content.query:
AppDependencies
| where Name == "agent.execute" and AppRoleName == "movate-runtime"
| extend project = tostring(Properties["project"])
| where isempty("{Project}") or project == "{Project}"
| summarize P95 = percentile(DurationMs, 95) by bin(TimeGenerated, 15m)
| order by TimeGenerated asc
content.title: agent.execute p95 latency (15m bins)

content.json:
## 6. Throughput by status (raw - works today)

**What.** `mdk.jobs.completed` summed by `status` over 15-minute bins (`AppMetrics`). Direct equivalent of the Grafana stacked-throughput panel; the ground truth behind error/dead-letter anomalies.

**Normal.** Steady `success`; `error` < 1%; `dead_letter` flat at zero.

**If red, do.** Any non-zero `dead_letter` -> `*-deadletter-spike` is or will fire; pull the failing run from section 4 and replay from Langfuse.

content.query:
AppMetrics
| where Name == "mdk.jobs.completed"
| extend status = tostring(Properties["status"]), project = tostring(Properties["project"])
| where isempty("{Project}") or project == "{Project}"
| summarize Jobs = sum(Sum) by status, bin(TimeGenerated, 15m)
| order by TimeGenerated asc
content.title: mdk.jobs.completed by status (15m bins)

content.json:
## 7. Cost trend (raw - works today)

**What.** `mdk.run.cost_usd` summed over 1-hour bins (`AppMetrics`), the ground truth behind the cost anomalies + the project-health cost trend sparkline.

**Normal.** Flat / proportional to throughput.

**If red, do.** Step-change with no token change -> provider price/model swap; runaway with token growth -> KB chunk-size regression or prompt drift; run `mdk eval` for the project's agents.

content.query:
AppMetrics
| where Name == "mdk.run.cost_usd"
| extend project = tostring(Properties["project"])
| where isempty("{Project}") or project == "{Project}"
| summarize CostUsd = sum(Sum) by bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| order by TimeGenerated asc
content.title: mdk.run.cost_usd per hour
